description: In 2013, Michael Dell and Silver Lake Partners initiated a $24.9 billion buyout to take Dell Inc. private to restructure away from the public markets' focus on quarterly results. The proposal faced fierce opposition from activist investor Carl Icahn, leading to a contentious shareholder battle over the company's valuation and long-term viability.

key_events.description: Announcement of the $13.65 per share buyout offer by Michael Dell and Silver Lake.
Carl Icahn launches a public proxy fight claiming the offer significantly undervalues the company.
Institutional Shareholder Services (ISS) recommends shareholders vote against the deal.
Dell board increases the dividend payout and amends voting rules to count abstentions as 'no' votes.
Shareholders approve the deal in a narrow vote after the company raises the offer slightly.

calibration_notes: This scenario captures the dynamics of an activist-led proxy battle against a founder-led buyout, highlighting the impact of voting rule changes and institutional advisor influence on final outcomes.
